calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jul-11-2024 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Business Record Subpoenas
Off Calendar Apr-04-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: PLAINTIFFS LORD PETRONI and JEFFREY NUSSBAUM's Motion To Quash Business Record Subpoenas.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
David W. Baer, Esq., of HARTOG, BAER, ZABRONSKY, (925) 253-1717, for Plaintiffs.
Daniel T. Bernhard, Esq., of FREELAND LAW LLP, (415) 541-0200, for Defendants.

Counsel for Plaintiff and counsel for Defendant stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem Judge. The pro tem Judge and counsels (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

The matter is argued. Upon conclusion of argument, Plaintiff's MOTION TO QUASH BUSINESS RECORD SUBPOENAS is denied.

The details of a proposed order are discussed in open court. Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare a proposed order, approved as to form, and email it to the judge pro tem at schwartz@cmslaw.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Andy Schwartz. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (302/JPT)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-21-2023 Set For Trial On Sep-23-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-28-2023 Continued To Oct-04-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendants And Cross Complainants' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Reassign And Transfer
Off Calendar Aug-14-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS MARCO PETRONI, PETER PETRONI'S Motion To Reassign And Transfer

Defendants' motion to reassign and transfer the case to the probate department is denied.

"If a motion is made for transfer of an action or proceeding to a different court location within the same superior court as provided in this section, proceedings shall be held as provided by local rules of the superior court." (CCP sec. 396a(f).) The Local Rule of San Francisco provides that this department hears all pretrial motions except for certain matters including "probate law and motion matters." (Local Rule 8.1(A)(1), 8.1(B)(7).)

Plaintiffs' complaint alleges breach of contract and account stated from a settlement agreement in a probate case. While the alleged settlement agreement involves probate, the settlement agreement is a contract similar to any of the other breach of contract cases routinely handled by this department. Further, the adjudication of this breach of contract claim requires application of contract principles, not the probate code.

Proposed order submitted.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
